model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Arkansas is the safer pick because its underlying profile is clearly stronger, with top-20 predictive rankings and an elite top-five offense in both major efficiency systems. Hawai'i has a credible upset path if its defense controls tempo and turns this into a lower-possession game, but the Razorbacks' overall talent, schedule strength, and scoring ceiling still make them the more reliable advance choice. I did not find a major availability concern strong enough to change that outlook.

reasoningStep.title: Arkansas over Hawai'i on overall team quality
reasoningStep.summary: Arkansas has the much stronger full-season efficiency profile, while Hawai'i's upset case depends on slowing the game and leaning on its defense.
evidence: Arkansas is No. 17 in KenPom and No. 20 in Torvik with a 25-8 record.
Arkansas ranks No. 5 nationally in offensive efficiency in both KenPom and Torvik.
Arkansas's defense is solid enough to support its elite offense, with Torvik rating it around No. 50 defensively.
Web preview results indicate Hawai'i finished 24-8 and is driven primarily by defense, with one preview citing the Rainbow Warriors around No. 42 in adjusted defensive efficiency.
Searches did not surface a major injury or availability issue for either team that would materially alter the matchup.

result.text: Arkansas vs. Hawaii: NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview | BettingPros to prevent flash of incorrect theme on page load --> Skip to main content Skip to navigation Skip to footer Click to go to homepage Picks Odds Tools Research Leaders Click to expand More menu Home Games Picks Upgrade Menu Click to Find a bet ... Contest Click to expand picks panel Click to open Sharp AI Arkansas vs. Hawaii: NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 The 2026 NCAA Tournament is officially here! The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament predictions and preview for Arkansas vs. Hawai’i. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview: Arkansas vs. Hawai’i Here are the odds for this opening-round matchup of the 2026 NCAA Tournament. Let’s dive into our preview and predictions for this NCAA Tournament matchup. Arkansas 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview John Calipari’s second year in Fayetteville has produced something genuinely special. Darius Acuff Jr. won both SEC Player of the Year and Freshman of the Year honors, becoming just the third player in conference history to sweep both awards, alongside Anthony Davis and Brandon Miller. That's good company. The freshman point guard averaged 22.2 points and 6.4 assists with a low turnover rate. He then backed it up with 37 points on 21 shot attempts against Oklahoma in the SEC Tournament quarterfinals to put any doubts about his ankle to rest. Texas coach Sean Miller called Acuff the best point guard he had ever been on a court with. That’s not hyperbole. Acuff is a generational offensive talent who can score from anywhere, create for others, and make the correct late-game read against any defense. If you don't know his name now, you will soon Trevon Brazile gives Arkansas a veteran safety valve in the frontcourt Brazile is a senior who delivered 28 points against Texas and hit a clutch overtime three to beat Missouri without Acuff in the lineup. Meleek Thomas is a fellow McDonald’s All-American freshman who gives this roster a second dangerous creator off the wing, shooting nearly 42% from deep. The Razorbacks take care of the ball at the best rate in the country with a 12% turnover rate, leading to an offense ranking top five in adjusted efficiency. Another interesting note is that no offense has been better in the second half of the season than Arkansas. Look for some second-half overs for any matchup Calipari's squad may find themselves in. You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
